lead_signal.headline: No new FIMI operations identified this week
lead_signal.body: Exhaustive search across Tier 1-3 sources yielded no new FIMI disclosures, platform takedowns, or government attributions published April 22-28, 2026. Most recent activity predates research window. Quiet week reflects disclosure timing rather than operational absence.

module_0.title: No new FIMI operations identified this week

vectors.name: Platform Opacity
vectors.headline: X/Twitter maintains no CIB disclosure program
vectors.summary: Structural gap creates systematic blind spot across all actors
vectors.detail: X/Twitter transparency gap persists with no coordinated inauthentic behavior disclosure equivalent to Meta CIB or Google TAG. Historical analysis shows networks persist on X post-attribution elsewhere. Quarterly disclosure cadence from Meta/Google creates weekly visibility gaps.
vectors.asymmetric: Platform opacity systematically under-documents FIMI activity scale

vectors.name: Attribution Gap
vectors.headline: Gulf/US/IL structural OSINT gaps persist
vectors.summary: Limited Tier 1-3 coverage for non-RU/CN actors
vectors.detail: Gulf states, US, and Israeli operations face persistent structural OSINT gaps. No regular Tier 1-3 source coverage. EEAS institutional perimeter excludes by design rather than evidentiary threshold.
vectors.asymmetric: Six-actor parity requires equal evidentiary standards despite coverage asymmetry

vectors.name: Regulatory Enforcement Gap
vectors.headline: DSA Article 40 implementation pending full effect
vectors.summary: Weekly granularity limited by quarterly platform reporting
vectors.detail: Tier 1 platforms publish quarterly/semiannually creating temporal gaps in FIMI monitoring. DSA FIMI provisions live but enforcement capacity building.
vectors.asymmetric: Regulatory cadence misaligned with operational tempo

module_9.title: Regulatory and Policy
module_9.friction_analysis: Highest regulatory friction remains X/Twitter non-participation in CIB disclosure standards and structural OSINT gaps for Gulf/US/IL actors. Quarterly platform reporting cadence misaligned with weekly operational monitoring needs.
module_9.dsa_fimi_status: DSA Article 40 FIMI provisions live but platform compliance varies. X/Twitter resistant; Meta/Google compliant via existing CIB programs. Enforcement capacity building phase with critical path through VLO designation and systemic risk assessments.
